thesis: Developing terrestrial Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) technology as a GPS backup — a genuine national security imperative. Pre-revenue company with 900 MHz spectrum license covering 90%+ of U.S. population. Binary outcome bet entirely dependent on FCC approval to monetize spectrum. Pinnacle service operational in 4,400 cities but tiny revenue. Critical catalyst: FCC Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPRM) could drop any day.

drivers: FCC NPRM drops with clear pathway for spectrum reconfiguration
Notice of Proposed Rulemaking validates regulatory approval
Government contracts for GPS backup system
Monopoly economics in critical 5G PNT infrastructure

risks: FCC denies or punts spectrum petition
Interagency delays from OMB or NTIA
Cash burn forces dilutive financing before approval
Qualcomm or Trimble develop competing solutions

base_case.assumptions: NPRM released with moderate pathway; 30-90 day comment period; Final approval 6-12 months after NPRM; Commercial deployment 2027; Revenue begins flowing 2027-2028.

catalysts.event: FCC NPRM Release — Binary catalyst (+20-40% or -40-60%)
Public Comment Period — Industry feedback on NPRM
Report and Order — Final FCC approval
Government Contract Awards — Validation and revenue

key_risks: Regulatory binary outcome — entirely dependent on FCC approval
Cash burn with 12-18 month runway; may need more capital
High 20%+ portfolio concentration dangerous for this risk profile
